      - heading "MiniCPM-SALA" [ref=e261] [level=2]
        - /url: "#minicpm-sala"
      - heading "Highlights" [ref=e262] [level=4]
        - /url: "#highlights"
      - paragraph: MiniCPM-SALA (Sparse Attention and Linear Attention) is the first large-scale hybrid model effectively integrating sparse and linear attention for million-token context modeling
      - paragraph: "✅ Innovative Hybrid Architecture: Synergizes 25% Sparse Attention (InfLLM-v2) for high-fidelity long context modeling with 75% Linear Attention (Lightning Attention) for global efficiency."
      - paragraph: "✅ Shattering Efficiency Walls: Breaks the \"Compute Wall\" and the \"Memory Wall,\" achieving 3.5× inference speed and significantly lower KV-cache overhead compared to dense baselines."
      - paragraph: "✅ Million-Token Context: Empowered by HyPE (Hybrid Positional Embedding), it scales to 1M+ tokens while maintaining strong length generalization."
      - paragraph: "✅ HALO Adaptation: Utilizes Hybrid Attention via Layer Optimization (HALO), a novel distillation recipe that effectively transfers dense attention capabilities to the hybrid architecture, avoiding the severe performance degradation typical of pure linear models."
      - heading "Introduction" [ref=e263] [level=4]
        - /url: "#introduction"
      - paragraph:
        - text: MiniCPM-SALA is an efficient hybrid model in which 25% of the layers adopt
        - link "InfLLM-V2" [ref=e264]:
          - /url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.24663
        - text: and the remaining 75% utilize Lightning Attention. This architecture enables inference of one million tokens on consumer GPUs such as the NVIDIA RTX 5090.
            - strong: SALA Hybrid Attention Mechanism
            - listitem: Integrates 25% InfLLM-V2 and 75% Lightning Attention, effectively leveraging the granular focus of sparse attention for local details and the high efficiency of linear attention for broad context.
            - strong: Transformer-to-Hybrid Continue Training
            - listitem: Circumvents the inefficiencies of cold-start training by performing an architectural transformation on the pre-trained weights, thereby reducing the total training budget to approximately 25% relative to training a comparable model from scratch.
        - listitem:
          - paragraph:
            - strong:
              - link "HyPE" [ref=e265]:
                - /url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.22156
              - text: (Hybrid Positional Encoding)
            - listitem: Harmonizes the performance across both short and long contexts, which can maintain general capabilities (e.g., knowledge, mathematics, and coding) comparable to modern full-attention models like Qwen3-8B and achieve substantial advantages across multiple long-context benchmarks.
            - strong: Efficient Inference on Long Sequences
            - listitem: Achieves up to 3.5x the inference speed of Qwen3-8B at a sequence length of 256K tokens on A6000D, supports inference at context lengths of up to 1M tokens on both NVIDIA A6000D and 5090 GPUs, whereas Qwen3-8B fails at this length due to out-of-memory (OOM) errors.
      - heading "Evaluation Results" [ref=e266] [level=3]
        - /url: "#evaluation-results"
      - heading "Efficiency Evaluation" [ref=e267] [level=4]
        - /url: "#efficiency-evaluation"
      - paragraph: "We benchmarked MiniCPM-SALA (9B) against Qwen3-8B on NVIDIA A6000D and RTX 5090 GPUs to evaluate inference speed and memory efficiency. The results demonstrate a significant performance leap: MiniCPM-SALA not only achieves up to a 2.5x speedup in time-to-first-token (TTFT) but also overcomes the memory bottlenecks of full-attention architectures. While Qwen3-8B suffers from OOM errors at extended lengths, MiniCPM-SALA successfully scales to 1M-token contexts on a single consumer-grade RTX 5090, effectively democratizing ultra-long context inference on edge hardware."
      - paragraph:
        - link "inference_speed_a6000d" [ref=e268]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm_sala/inference_speed_a600d.png
      - paragraph:
        - link "inference_speed_5090" [ref=e269]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm_sala/inference_speed_5090.png
      - heading "Long-Context Evaluation" [ref=e270] [level=4]
        - /url: "#long-context-evaluation"
      - paragraph: MiniCPM-SALA consistently outperforms other open-source LLMs of similar scale across most involved long-context benchmarks. Specifically, it achieves the highest scores in the RULER and NoLiMa tests at all context lengths (up to 128K) and maintains the highest overall average score of 38.97, suggesting superior performance in handling long-context information processing.
      - paragraph:
        - link "long_text_evaluation" [ref=e271]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm_sala/long_text_evaluation.png
      - heading "Ultra-long Context Evaluation" [ref=e272] [level=4]
        - /url: "#ultra-long-context-evaluation"
      - paragraph: The evaluation demonstrates that MiniCPM-SALA exhibits effective length extrapolation capabilities, maintaining a score of 81.6 at a 2048K context length despite being trained on only 520K tokens. The model achieves this without auxiliary techniques like YaRN, likely due to its NoPE configuration in sparse attention layers.
      - paragraph:
        - link "ultra_long_text_evaluation" [ref=e273]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm_sala/ultra_long_text_evaluation.png
      - heading "Standard Evaluation" [ref=e274] [level=4]
        - /url: "#standard-evaluation"
      - paragraph: MiniCPM-SALA achieves an average score of 76.53 across standard benchmarks, outperforming comparable models such as Qwen3-8B and Falcon-H1R-7B. The architecture maintains robust performance in Knowledge, Code, and Math.
      - paragraph:
        - link "benchmark" [ref=e275]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm_sala/benchmark.png
      - heading "Inference" [ref=e276] [level=3]
        - /url: "#inference"
        - text: To achieve optimal performance, we recommend using the
        - code: Temperature=0.9
        - text: .
      - heading "HuggingFace" [ref=e277] [level=4]
        - /url: "#huggingface"
      - paragraph: "Our model is readily compatible with 🤗 Hugging Face transformers. You can perform inference with our model as follows:"
      - text: "import torch from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer model_path = \"openbmb/MiniCPM-SALA\" tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_path) model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_path, trust_remote_code=True, device_map=\"auto\") model.eval() prompts = [\"My name is\", \"The capital of China is\"] with torch.no_grad(): inputs = tokenizer(prompts, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(model.device) outputs = model.generate(**inputs) output_texts = tokenizer.batch_decode(outputs) print(output_texts)"

      - heading "MiniCPM4 and MiniCPM4.1 Series" [ref=e306] [level=2]
        - /url: "#minicpm4-and-minicpm41-series"
      - link [ref=e307] [nth=1]:

      - heading "Highlights" [ref=e308] [nth=1] [level=4]
        - /url: "#highlights-1"
      - paragraph: "MiniCPM 4.1-8B is the first open-source reasoning LLM with trainable sparse attention:"
      - paragraph: "✅ Strong Reasoning Capability: Surpasses similar-sized models on 15 tasks!"
      - paragraph: "✅ Fast Generation: 3x decoding speedup for reasoning"
      - paragraph: "✅ Efficient Architecture: Trainable sparse attention, frequency-ranked speculative decoding"
      - heading "Introduction" [ref=e309] [nth=1] [level=4]
        - /url: "#introduction-1"
      - paragraph: "MiniCPM4 and MiniCPM4.1 series are highly efficient large language models (LLMs) designed explicitly for end-side devices, which achieves this efficiency through systematic innovation in four key dimensions: model architecture, training data, training algorithms, and inference systems."
        - listitem:
          - paragraph:
            - text: 🏗️
            - strong: "Efficient Model Architecture:"
            - listitem:
              - text: "InfLLM-V2 -- Trainable Sparse Attention Mechanism: Adopts a trainable sparse attention mechanism architecture where each token only needs to compute relevance with less than 5% of tokens in 128K long text processing, significantly reducing computational overhead for long texts ("
              - link "InfLLM-V2 Training Kernels" [ref=e310]:
                - /url: https://github.com/OpenBMB/infllmv2_cuda_impl
              - text: )
            - text: 🧠
            - strong: "Efficient Learning Algorithms:"
            - listitem: "Model Wind Tunnel 2.0 -- Efficient Predictable Scaling: Introduces scaling prediction methods for performance of downstream tasks, enabling more precise model training configuration search"
            - listitem: "BitCPM -- Ultimate Ternary Quantization: Compresses model parameter bit-width to 3 values, achieving 90% extreme model bit-width reduction"
            - listitem: "Efficient Training Engineering Optimization: Adopts FP8 low-precision computing technology combined with Multi-token Prediction training strategy"
        - listitem:
          - paragraph:
            - text: 📚
            - strong: "High-Quality Training Data:"
            - listitem:
              - text: "UltraClean -- High-quality Pre-training Data Filtering and Generation: Builds iterative data cleaning strategies based on efficient data verification, open-sourcing high-quality Chinese and English pre-training dataset"
              - link "UltraFinweb" [ref=e311]:
                - /url: https://huggingface.co/datasets/openbmb/Ultra-FineWeb
            - listitem: "UltraChat v2 -- High-quality Supervised Fine-tuning Data Generation: Constructs large-scale high-quality supervised fine-tuning datasets covering multiple dimensions including knowledge-intensive data, reasoning-intensive data, instruction-following data, long text understanding data, and tool calling data"
        - listitem:
          - paragraph:
            - text: ⚡
            - strong: "Efficient Inference and Deployment System:"
            - listitem:
              - text: "CPM.cu -- Lightweight and Efficient CUDA Inference Framework: Integrates sparse attention, model quantization, and speculative sampling to achieve efficient prefilling and decoding ("
              - link "Inference Kernels and Framework" [ref=e312]:
                - /url: https://github.com/openbmb/cpm.cu
              - text: )
            - listitem: "ArkInfer -- Cross-platform Deployment System: Supports efficient deployment across multiple backend environments, providing flexible cross-platform adaptation capabilities"
      - heading "Evaluation Results" [ref=e313] [nth=1] [level=3]
        - /url: "#evaluation-results-1"
      - heading "Efficiency Evaluation" [ref=e314] [nth=1] [level=4]
        - /url: "#efficiency-evaluation-1"
      - paragraph: On two typical end-side chips, Jetson AGX Orin and RTX 4090, MiniCPM4 and MiniCPM4.1 demonstrate significantly faster processing speed compared to similar-size models in long text processing tasks. As text length increases, MiniCPM4 and MiniCPM4.1's efficiency advantage becomes more pronounced. On the Jetson AGX Orin platform, compared to Qwen3-8B, MiniCPM4 and MiniCPM4.1 achieves approximately 7x decoding speed improvement.
      - paragraph:
        - link "benchmark" [ref=e315] [nth=1]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm4/efficiency.png
      - paragraph: MiniCPM4.1 achieves 3x decoding speed improvement in reasoning.
      - paragraph:
        - link "benchmark" [ref=e316] [nth=2]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm4/minicpm4.1_speed.png
      - heading "Comprehensive Evaluation" [ref=e317] [level=4]
        - /url: "#comprehensive-evaluation"
      - paragraph: MiniCPM4 launches end-side versions with 8B and 0.5B parameter scales, both achieving best-in-class performance in their respective categories.
      - paragraph:
        - link "benchmark" [ref=e318] [nth=3]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm4/benchmark.png
      - paragraph: MiniCPM4.1 launches end-side versions with 8B parameter scale, achieving best-in-class performance in deep reasoning mode.
      - paragraph:
        - link "benchmark" [ref=e319] [nth=4]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm4/benchmark4.1.png
      - heading "Long Text Evaluation" [ref=e320] [level=4]
        - /url: "#long-text-evaluation"
      - paragraph: MiniCPM4 is pre-trained on 32K long texts and achieves length extension through YaRN technology. In the 128K long text needle-in-a-haystack task, MiniCPM4 demonstrates outstanding performance. MiniCPM4.1 is pre-trained on 64K long texts and achieves length extension through YaRN technology. In the 128K long text needle-in-a-haystack task, MiniCPM4.1 demonstrates outstanding performance.
      - paragraph:
        - link "long-niah" [ref=e321]:
          - /url: /OpenBMB/MiniCPM/blob/main/assets/minicpm4/128k-niah.png
      - heading "Inference" [ref=e322] [nth=1] [level=3]
        - /url: "#inference-1"
      - paragraph: "MiniCPM 4.1 can be used with following frameworks: Huggingface Transformers, SGLang, vLLM, and CPM.cu. For the ultimate inference speed, we highly recommend CPM.cu."
      - paragraph: MiniCPM4/MiniCPM4.1 supports both dense attention inference and sparse attention inference modes, where vLLM and SGLang currently only support dense inference mode. If you want to use sparse inference mode, please use Huggingface Transformers and CPM.cu.
        - listitem: "Dense attention inference: vLLM, SGLang, Huggingface Transformers"
        - listitem: "Sparse attention inference: Huggingface Transformers, CPM.cu"
      - heading "Hybird Reasoning Mode" [ref=e323] [level=4]
        - /url: "#hybird-reasoning-mode"
        - text: MiniCPM4.1 supports hybrid reasoning mode, which can be used in both deep reasoning mode and non-reasoning mode. To enable hybrid reasoning mode. User can set
        - code: enable_thinking=True
        - text: in
        - code: tokenizer.apply_chat_template
        - text: to enable hybrid reasoning mode, and set
        - code: enable_thinking=False
        - text: to enable non-reasoning mode. Similarly, user can directly add
        - code: /no_think
        - text: at the end of the query to enable non-reasoning mode. If not add any special token or add
        - code: /think
        - text: at the end of the query, the model will enable reasoning mode.
      - text: "# Enable reasoning mode prompt_text = tokenizer.apply_chat_template( messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=True, enable_thinking=True ) # Enable non-reasoning mode prompt_text = tokenizer.apply_chat_template( messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=True, enable_thinking=False )"
      - button "Copy" [ref=e324] [nth=4]
      - heading "HuggingFace" [ref=e325] [nth=1] [level=4]
        - /url: "#huggingface-1"
          - strong: Inference with Dense Attention
      - text: "from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer import torch torch.manual_seed(0) path = 'openbmb/MiniCPM4.1-8B' device = \"cuda\" tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(path) model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(path, torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16, device_map=device, trust_remote_code=True) # User can directly use the chat interface # responds, history = model.chat(tokenizer, \"Write an article about Artificial Intelligence.\", temperature=0.7, top_p=0.7) # print(responds) # User can also use the generate interface messages = [ {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Write an article about Artificial Intelligence.\"}, ] prompt_text = tokenizer.apply_chat_template( messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=True, ) model_inputs = tokenizer([prompt_text], return_tensors=\"pt\").to(device) model_outputs = model.generate( **model_inputs, max_new_tokens=32768, top_p=0.95, temperature=0.6 ) output_token_ids = [ model_outputs[i][len(model_inputs[i]):] for i in range(len(model_inputs['input_ids'])) ] responses = tokenizer.batch_decode(output_token_ids, skip_special_tokens=True)[0] print(responses)"
      - button "Copy" [ref=e326] [nth=5]
        - listitem:
          - strong: Inference with Sparse Attention
          - text: This model supports InfLLM v2, a sparse attention mechanism designed for efficient long-sequence inference. It requires the
          - link "infllmv2_cuda_impl" [ref=e327]:
            - /url: https://github.com/OpenBMB/infllmv2_cuda_impl
          - text: library.
      - paragraph: "You can install it by running the following command:"
      - text: "git clone -b feature_infer https://github.com/OpenBMB/infllmv2_cuda_impl.git cd infllmv2_cuda_impl git submodule update --init --recursive pip install -e . # or python setup.py install"
      - button "Copy" [ref=e328] [nth=6]
        - text: To enable InfLLM v2, you need to add the
        - code: sparse_config
        - text: field in
        - code: config.json
        - text: ":"
      - text: "{ ..., \"sparse_config\": { \"kernel_size\": 32, \"kernel_stride\": 16, \"init_blocks\": 1, \"block_size\": 64, \"window_size\": 2048, \"topk\": 64, \"use_nope\": false, \"dense_len\": 8192 } }"
      - button "Copy" [ref=e329] [nth=7]
      - paragraph: "These parameters control the behavior of InfLLM v2:"
          - code: kernel_size
          - text: "(default: 32): The size of semantic kernels."
          - code: kernel_stride
          - text: "(default: 16): The stride between adjacent kernels."
          - code: init_blocks
          - text: "(default: 1): The number of initial blocks that every query token attends to. This ensures attention to the beginning of the sequence."
          - code: block_size
          - text: "(default: 64): The block size for key-value blocks."
          - code: window_size
          - text: "(default: 2048): The size of the local sliding window."
          - code: topk
          - text: "(default: 64): The specifies that each token computes attention with only the top-k most relevant key-value blocks."
          - code: use_nope
          - text: "(default: false): Whether to use the NOPE technique in block selection for improved performance."
          - code: dense_len
          - text: "(default: 8192): Since Sparse Attention offers limited benefits for short sequences, the model can use standard (dense) attention for shorter texts. The model will use dense attention for sequences with a token length below"
          - code: dense_len
          - text: and switch to sparse attention for sequences exceeding this length. Set this to
          - code: "-1"
          - text: to always use sparse attention regardless of sequence length.
          - strong: Long Context Extension
          - text: MiniCPM4.1 natively supports context lengths of up to 65,536(64k) tokens. For conversations where the total length (including both input and output) significantly exceeds this limit, we recommend using RoPE scaling techniques for effective handling of long texts. We have validated the model's performance on context lengths of up to 131,072 tokens by modifying the LongRoPE factor.

        - code: config.json

      - paragraph: "For accelerated inference with speculative decoding using vLLM, follow these steps:"
      - heading "1. Download MiniCPM4.1 Draft Model" [ref=e333] [level=6]
        - /url: "#1-download-minicpm41-draft-model"
      - paragraph: "First, download the MiniCPM4.1 draft model:"
      - text: cd /your_path git clone https://huggingface.co/openbmb/MiniCPM4.1-8B-Eagle3
      - button "Copy" [ref=e334] [nth=9]
      - heading "2. Install EAGLE3-Compatible vLLM" [ref=e335] [level=6]
        - /url: "#2-install-eagle3-compatible-vllm"
      - paragraph: "The EAGLE3 vLLM PR has been submitted. For now, use our repository for installation:"
      - text: git clone https://github.com/LDLINGLINGLING/vllm.git cd vllm pip install -e .
      - button "Copy" [ref=e336] [nth=10]
      - heading "3. Launch vLLM Server with Speculative Decoding" [ref=e337] [level=6]
        - /url: "#3-launch-vllm-server-with-speculative-decoding"
      - paragraph: "Start the vLLM inference server with speculative decoding enabled. Make sure to update the model path in the speculative-config to point to your downloaded MiniCPM4_1-8B-Eagle3-bf16 folder:"
      - text: "VLLM_USE_V1=1 \\ vllm serve openbmb/MiniCPM4.1-8B \\ --seed 42 \\ --trust-remote-code \\ --speculative-config '{ \"model\": \"your/path/MiniCPM4_1-8B-Eagle3-bf16\", \"num_speculative_tokens\": 3, \"method\": \"eagle3\", \"draft_tensor_parallel_size\": 1 }'"
      - button "Copy" [ref=e338] [nth=11]
      - heading "4. Client Usage Example" [ref=e339] [level=6]
        - /url: "#4-client-usage-example"
      - paragraph: "The client usage remains the same for both standard and speculative decoding:"
      - text: "import openai client = openai.Client(base_url=\"http://localhost:8000/v1\", api_key=\"EMPTY\") response = client.chat.completions.create( model=\"openbmb/MiniCPM4.1-8B\", messages=[ {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Write an article about Artificial Intelligence.\"}, ], temperature=0.6, max_tokens=32768, extra_body=dict(add_special_tokens=True), # Ensures special tokens are added for chat template ) print(response.choices[0].message.content)"
      - button "Copy" [ref=e340] [nth=12]
      - heading "vLLM Configuration Parameters" [ref=e341] [level=6]

              - text: ": Tensor parallel size for draft model (default: 1)"
          - code: "--seed"
          - text: ": Random seed for reproducibility"
          - code: "--trust-remote-code"

      - heading "Standard Inference (Without Speculative Decoding)" [ref=e342] [level=5]
        - /url: "#standard-inference-without-speculative-decoding"
      - paragraph: For now, you need to install the latest version of vLLM.
      - text: pip install -U vllm \ --pre \ --extra-index-url https://wheels.vllm.ai/nightly
      - button "Copy" [ref=e343] [nth=13]
      - paragraph: "Then you can inference MiniCPM4.1-8B with vLLM:"
      - text: "from transformers import AutoTokenizer from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams model_name = \"openbmb/MiniCPM4.1-8B\" prompt = [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Write an article about Artificial Intelligence.\"}] tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name, trust_remote_code=True) input_text = tokenizer.apply_chat_template(prompt, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=True) llm = LLM( model=model_name, trust_remote_code=True, max_num_batched_tokens=65536, dtype=\"bfloat16\", gpu_memory_utilization=0.8, ) sampling_params = SamplingParams(top_p=0.95, temperature=0.6, max_tokens=32768) outputs = llm.generate(prompts=input_text, sampling_params=sampling_params) print(outputs[0].outputs[0].text)"
      - button "Copy" [ref=e344] [nth=14]
      - paragraph: "Also, you can start the inference server by running the following command:"
          - strong: Note
          - text: ": In vLLM's chat API,"
          - code: add_special_tokens
          - text: is
          - code: "False"
          - text: by default. This means important special tokens—such as the beginning-of-sequence (BOS) token—will not be added automatically. To ensure the input prompt is correctly formatted for the model, you should explicitly set
          - code: "extra_body={\"add_special_tokens\": True}"
          - text: .
      - text: vllm serve openbmb/MiniCPM4.1-8B --trust-remote-code

        - strong: MiniCPM-o
        - text: is the latest series of on-device multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) ungraded from MiniCPM-V. The models can now take image, video, text, and audio as inputs and provide high-quality text and speech outputs in an end-to-end fashion. The model series is designed for
        - strong: strong performance and efficient deployment
        - text: ". The most notable models in the series currently include:"
            - strong: MiniCPM-o 4.5
            - text: ": 🔥🔥🔥 The latest and most capable model in the series. With a total of 9B parameters, this end-to-end model"
            - strong: approaches Gemini 2.5 Flash in vision, speech, and full-duplex multimodal live streaming
            - text: ", making it one of the most versatile and performant models in the open-source community. The new full-duplex multimodal live streaming capability means that the output streams (speech and text), and the real-time input streams (video and audio) do not block each other. This"
            - strong: enables MiniCPM-o 4.5 to see, listen, and speak simultaneously
            - text: in a real-time omnimodal conversation, and perform
            - strong: proactive interactions
            - text: such as proactive reminding. The improved voice mode supports bilingual real-time speech conversation in a more natural, expressive, and stable way, and also allows for voice cloning. It also advances MiniCPM-V's visual capabilities such as strong OCR capability, trustworthy behavior and multilingual support, etc. We also rollout a
            - strong: high-performing llama.cpp-omni inference framework together with a WebRTC Demo
            - text: ", to bring this full-duplex multimodal live streaming experience available on local devices such as Macs."
            - strong: MiniCPM-V 4.0
            - text: ": ⭐️⭐️⭐️ An efficient model in the MiniCPM-V series. With a total of 4B parameters, the model surpasses GPT-4.1-mini-20250414 in image understanding on the OpenCompass evaluation. With its small parameter size and efficient architecture, MiniCPM-V 4.0 is an ideal choice for on-device deployment on the phone."

      - heading "MiniCPM-o 4.5" [ref=e132] [level=2]
        - /url: "#minicpm-o-45"
        - strong: MiniCPM-o 4.5
        - text: "is the latest and most capable model in the MiniCPM-o series. The model is built in an end-to-end fashion based on SigLip2, Whisper-medium, CosyVoice2, and Qwen3-8B with a total of 9B parameters. It exhibits a significant performance improvement, and introduces new features for full-duplex multimodal live streaming. Notable features of MiniCPM-o 4.5 include:"
            - text: 🔥
            - strong: Leading Visual Capability.
            - text: MiniCPM-o 4.5 achieves an average score of 77.6 on OpenCompass, a comprehensive evaluation of 8 popular benchmarks.
            - strong: With only 9B parameters, it surpasses widely used proprietary models like GPT-4o, Gemini 2.0 Pro, and approaches Gemini 2.5 Flash
            - text: for vision-language capabilities. It supports instruct and thinking modes in a single model, better covering efficiency and performance trade-offs in different user scenarios.
            - text: 🎙
            - strong: Strong Speech Capability.
            - text: MiniCPM-o 4.5 supports
            - strong: bilingual real-time speech conversation with configurable voices
            - text: in English and Chinese. It features
            - strong: more natural, expressive and stable speech conversation
            - text: . The model also allows for fun features such as
            - strong: voice cloning and role play via a simple reference audio clip
            - text: ", where the cloning performance surpasses strong TTS tools such as CosyVoice2."
            - text: 🎬
            - strong: New Full-Duplex and Proactive Multimodal Live Streaming Capability.
            - text: As a new feature, MiniCPM-o 4.5 can process real-time, continuous video and audio input streams simultaneously while generating concurrent text and speech output streams in an end-to-end fashion, without mutual blocking. This
            - strong: allows MiniCPM-o 4.5 to see, listen, and speak simultaneously
            - text: ", creating a fluid, real-time omnimodal conversation experience. Beyond reactive responses, the model can also perform"
            - strong: proactive interaction
            - text: ", such as initiating reminders or comments based on its continuous understanding of the live scene."
            - text: 💪
            - strong: Strong OCR Capability, Efficiency and Others.
            - text: Advancing popular visual capabilities from MiniCPM-V series, MiniCPM-o 4.5 can process
            - strong: high-resolution images
            - text: (up to 1.8 million pixels) and
            - strong: high-FPS videos
            - text: (up to 10fps) in any aspect ratio efficiently. It achieves
            - strong: state-of-the-art performance for end-to-end English document parsing
            - text: on OmniDocBench, outperforming proprietary models such as Gemini-3 Flash and GPT-5, and specialized tools such as DeepSeek-OCR 2. It also features
            - strong: trustworthy behaviors
            - text: ", matching Gemini 2.5 Flash on MMHal-Bench, and supports"
            - strong: multilingual capabilities
            - text: on more than 30 languages.
            - text: 💫
            - strong: Easy Usage.
            - text: "MiniCPM-o 4.5 can be easily used in various ways:"
            - strong: "Basic usage, recommended for 100% precision:"
            - text: PyTorch inference with Nvidia GPU.
            - strong: Other end-side adaptation
            - text: includes (1) llama.cpp and Ollama support for efficient CPU inference on local devices, (2) int4 and GGUF format quantized models in 16 sizes, (3) vLLM and SGLang support for high-throughput and memory-efficient inference, (4) FlagOS support for the unified multi-chip backend plugin.
            - strong: We also open-sourced web demos
            - text: on which
            - strong: enables the full-duplex multimodal live streaming experience on local devices
            - text: such as GPUs, PCs (e.g., on a MacBook).
        - strong: Model Architecture.
          - strong: End-to-end Omni-modal Architecture.
          - text: The modality encoders/decoders and LLM are densely connected via hidden states in an end-to-end fashion. This enables better information flow and control, and also facilitates full exploitation of rich multimodal knowledge during training.
          - strong: Full-Duplex Omni-modal Live Streaming Mechanism.
          - text: (1) We turn the offline modality encoder/decoders into online and full-duplex ones for streaming inputs/outputs. The speech token decoder models text and speech tokens in an interleaved fashion to support full-duplex speech generation (i.e., sync timely with new input). This also facilitates more stable long speech generation (e.g., > 1min). (2)
          - strong: We sync all the input and output streams on timeline in milliseconds
          - text: ", which are jointly modeled by a time-division multiplexing (TDM) mechanism for omni-modality streaming processing in the LLM backbone. It divides parallel omni-modality streams into sequential info groups within small periodic time slices."
          - strong: Proactive Interaction Mechanism.
          - text: The LLM continuously monitors the input video and audio streams, and decides at a frequency of 1Hz to speak or not. This high decision-making frequency together with full-duplex nature are curcial to enable the proactive interaction capability.
          - strong: Configurable Speech Modeling Design.
          - text: We inherent the multimodal system prompt design of MiniCPM-o 2.6, which includes a traditional text system prompt, and a new audio system prompt to determine the assistant voice. This enables cloning new voices and role play in inference time for speech conversation.

      - paragraph: "Requirements:"

      - heading "Supported Frameworks" [ref=e169] [level=2]

      - heading "FlagOS" [ref=e170] [level=3]
        - /url: "#flagos"
      - paragraph: To enable large-scale deployment across different AI chips, Beijing Zhiyuan Research Institute, together with numerous research institutions, chip manufacturers, system vendors, and algorithm and software organizations both domestically and internationally, jointly initiated and established the FlagOS Open Source Community.
      - paragraph:
        - text: "The FlagOS community is dedicated to building a unified, open-source system software stack for various AI chips, encompassing core open-source projects such as a large-scale operator library, a unified AI compiler, parallel training and inference frameworks, and a unified communication library. It aims to create an open technology ecosystem connecting the “model-system-chip” layers. By enabling “develop once, deploy across chips”, FlagOS unlocks the computational potential of hardware, breaks down the ecosystem silos between different chip software stacks, and effectively reduces migration costs for developers. The FlagOS community fosters an AI hardware and software ecosystem, overcomes single-vendor closed-source monopolies, promotes widespread deployment of AI hardware technologies, and is committed to rooted in China while embracing global collaboration. Official website:"
        - link "https://flagos.io" [ref=e171]:
          - /url: https://flagos.io
        - text: .

      - heading "MiniCPM-V 4.0" [ref=e195] [level=2]
        - /url: "#minicpm-v-40"
      - paragraph:
        - strong: MiniCPM-V 4.0
        - text: "is an efficient model in the MiniCPM-V series. The model is built based on SigLIP2-400M and MiniCPM4-3B with a total of 4.1B parameters. It inherits the strong single-image, multi-image and video understanding performance of MiniCPM-V 2.6 with largely improved efficiency. Notable features of MiniCPM-V 4.0 include:"
            - text: 🔥
            - strong: Leading Visual Capability.
            - text: With only 4.1B parameters, MiniCPM-V 4.0 achieves an average score of 69.0 on OpenCompass, a comprehensive evaluation of 8 popular benchmarks,
            - strong: outperforming GPT-4.1-mini-20250414, MiniCPM-V 2.6 (8.1B params, OpenCompass 65.2) and Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct (3.8B params, OpenCompass 64.5)
            - text: . It also shows good performance in multi-image understanding and video understanding.
            - text: 🚀
            - strong: Superior Efficiency.
            - text: Designed for on-device deployment, MiniCPM-V 4.0 runs smoothly on end devices. For example, it delivers
            - strong: less than 2s first token delay and more than 17 token/s decoding on iPhone 16 Pro Max
            - text: ", without heating problems. It also shows superior throughput under concurrent requests."
        - listitem:
          - paragraph:
            - text: 💫
            - strong: Easy Usage.
            - text: MiniCPM-V 4.0 can be easily used in various ways including
            - strong: llama.cpp, Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, LLaMA-Factory and local web demo
            - text: etc. We also open-source iOS App that can run on iPhone and iPad. Get started easily with our well-structured
            - link "Cookbook" [ref=e196] [nth=3]:
              - /url: https://github.com/OpenSQZ/MiniCPM-V-CookBook
            - text: ", featuring detailed instructions and practical examples."

      - heading "Limitations" [ref=e293] [level=2]
        - /url: "#limitations"
      - paragraph: As an experimental trial, we find MiniCPM-o 4.5 has notable limitations worth further investigation and improvement.
          - strong: Foundation Capability.
          - text: The full-duplex omni-modality live streaminig capability still needs improvement in its foundation capability.
          - strong: Unstable Speech Output in Omni Mode.
          - text: Speech synthesis can mispronounce characters in full-duplex omni-modal live streaminig mode.
          - strong: Mixed Language.
          - text: The model can sometimes respond with mixed English and Chinese in speech and omni mode.
          - strong: High-latency on Web Demo.
          - text: Users may experience unusual high-latency or even miss part of model output fragments when using our web demo hosted on overseas servers. We recommend deploying the demo locally or with good network connections.

# MiniCPM 调研报告：端侧多模态大模型的演进与突破

**调研日期**：2026年3月29日  
**数据来源**：GitHub / Hugging Face / ModelScope / 官方技术文档

---

## 一、项目概览

### 1.1 什么是 MiniCPM

MiniCPM 是由**清华大学 THUNLP 实验室**、**中国人民大学高灵学院（Gaoling School of AI）**与**模型最佳科技（ModelBest）**联合开发的**轻量级多模态大模型系列**。项目托管于 GitHub（[OpenBMB/MiniCPM](https://github.com/OpenBMB/MiniCPM)），采用 Apache-2.0 开源许可证，**免费可商用**。

MiniCPM 的核心理念是：**将 GPT-4V 级别的多模态能力压缩到能在手机端离线运行的模型体积**。从 2024 年 2 月发布首个 2B 参数版本至今，MiniCPM 已演化为一个涵盖纯语言模型（LLM）、视觉-语言模型（VLM）、端到端多模态模型（MiniCPM-o）的完整家族。

> 📌 **里程碑时间线**
> - 2024.02 — MiniCPM-2B 发布，性能对标 Mistral-7B
> - 2024.04 — 发布 MiniCPM-2B-128K、MiniCPM-MoE-8x2B、MiniCPM-1B
> - 2024.07 — 发布 MiniCPM-S-1B（87.89% FFN 稀疏度）
> - 2024.09 — 发布 MiniCPM3-4B（超越 Phi-3.5-mini，逼近 Llama3.1-8B）
> - 2025.06 — 发布 MiniCPM4（端侧 5 倍生成加速）
> - 2025.09 — 发布 MiniCPM4.1 系列（混合推理 + 可训练稀疏注意力）
> - 2026.02 — 发布 MiniCPM-SALA（100 万 token 上下文，稀疏+线性注意力混合架构）
> - 2025.01/08 — 陆续发布 MiniCPM-o 2.6 / 4.5（端到端语音-视觉-文本多模态）

---

## 二、核心系列详解

### 2.1 MiniCPM 4 系列（语言模型）

#### MiniCPM4-8B / MiniCPM4-0.5B

MiniCPM4 是专为**端侧设备**优化的 LLM 系列，通过四个维度的系统创新实现效率突破：

| 维度 | 技术名称 | 说明 |
|------|---------|------|
| 模型架构 | **InfLLM-V2** 可训练稀疏注意力 | 在 128K 长文本中，每个 token 仅需计算与不到 5% token 的相关性 |
| 学习算法 | Model Wind Tunnel 2.0（高效可预测扩展）、BitCPM（三元量化，1.58 bit） | FP8 低精度计算 + Multi-token Prediction |
| 训练数据 | UltraClean（高质量预训练数据过滤）、UltraChat v2（高质量 SFT 数据） | 覆盖知识密集、推理、指令跟随、长文本等多维度 |
| 推理部署 | CPM.cu（轻量 CUDA 推理框架）、ArkInfer（跨平台部署） | 稀疏注意力 + 量化 + 投机采样协同 |

**性能亮点**：
- 在 Jetson AGX Orin 上，相比 Qwen3-8B 实现**约 7 倍解码速度提升**
- 在典型端侧芯片上，生成速度提升**超过 5 倍**
- 标准评测基准与同尺寸最优模型持平

#### MiniCPM4.1-8B（最新旗舰语言模型）

MiniCPM4.1 是**首个开源的可训练稀疏注意力推理 LLM**，支持**混合推理模式**（深度推理 / 非推理模式切换）。

**核心特性**：
- ✅ 稀疏注意力推理：每 token 只需计算少量相关性，大幅降低长文本计算开销
- ✅ 混合推理模式：通过 `enable_thinking=True/False` 或在对话末尾加 `/think` / `/no_think` 动态切换
- ✅ 深度推理模式：**3 倍解码加速**（相比标准推理）
- ✅ 支持上下文最长 64K，原生支持 LongRoPE 扩展至 128K

**推理框架**：HuggingFace Transformers / vLLM / SGLang / **CPM.cu**（官方推荐，用于稀疏推理）

#### BitCPM4 系列（三元量化）

BitCPM4 通过**量化感知训练（QAT）**将模型参数压缩至仅 **1.58 bit**（3 值），性能与全精度同尺寸模型相当，同时训练效率大幅提升。

---

### 2.2 MiniCPM-SALA（前沿稀疏架构，2026.02 最新发布）

**MiniCPM-SALA** 是 2026 年 2 月 11 日发布的重磅新模型，全称 **Sparse Attention and Linear Attention**，是**首个有效融合稀疏注意力和线性注意力**的大规模混合模型，专为百万 token 上下文场景设计。

#### 架构创新

```
MiniCPM-SALA 混合注意力分布：
  ▓▓▓░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  25% — InfLLM-V2（稀疏注意力）
  ████████████████████████████████░░░░  75% — Lightning Attention（线性注意力）
```

- **25% 稀疏注意力层**（InfLLM-V2）：保留高精度局部细节建模
- **75% 线性注意力层**（Lightning Attention）：全局高效计算
- **HyPE（Hybrid Positional Embedding）**：协调短上下文和长上下文性能
- **HALO Adaptation**：通过层优化将密集注意力能力蒸馏到混合架构

#### 性能数据

**效率评测（A6000D / RTX 5090）**：

| 指标 | MiniCPM-SALA (9B) | Qwen3-8B（对比） |
|------|------------------|-----------------|
| 首 token 时间（TTFT） | 最高 2.5 倍加速 | baseline |
| 128K 上下文推理速度 | 3.5 倍加速 | OOM 失败 |
| 1M token 上下文 | ✅ RTX 5090 单卡运行 | ❌ OOM |
| RULER / NoLiMa（128K） | 最高分 | 低于 SALA |
| 2048K 超长上下文得分 | **81.6**（训练仅用 520K token） | 不支持 |

**标准评测基准（平均分）**：

| 模型 | 平均分 |
|------|--------|
| **MiniCPM-SALA** | **76.53** ✅ |
| Qwen3-8B | 略低 |
| Falcon-H1R-7B | 低于 SALA |

> 🔥 **SOAR 2026 大赛**（稀疏算子加速挑战赛）正在进行，NVIDIA 6000D 环境，奖金池超 10 万美元，目标是优化 9B 规模、100 万 token 推理性能。

---

### 2.3 MiniCPM-V 系列（视觉-语言模型）

#### MiniCPM-V 4.0（2025 年最新图像理解模型）

基于 **SigLIP2-400M 视觉编码器 + MiniCPM4-3B 语言基座**，总参数仅 **4.1B**。

**核心指标**：
- OpenCompass 8 大基准平均得分：**69.0**（超越 GPT-4.1-mini-20250414、MiniCPM-V 2.6、Qwen2.5-VL-3B）
- **iPhone 16 Pro Max** 实测：首 token 延迟 < 2秒，解码速度 > 17 token/s，无发热问题
- 支持 llama.cpp / Ollama / vLLM / SGLang / LLaMA-Factory / 本地 Web Demo
- 已开源 iOS App，支持 iPhone / iPad 离线运行

#### MiniCPM-V 4.5（超越 GPT-4o 的视觉能力，2025.08）

**核心指标**：
- OpenCompass 平均分：**77.6**
- 在多项基准上**超越 GPT-4o-latest、Gemini-2.0 Pro、Qwen2.5-VL 72B**
- 支持高分辨率图像（最高 180 万像素）、高帧率视频（最高 10fps，任意宽高比）
- OmniDocBench 端到端英文文档解析：**超越 Gemini-3 Flash、GPT-5、DeepSeek-OCR 2**
- 支持 30+ 语言多语言能力
- 可信行为（MMHal-Bench）：**与 Gemini 2.5 Flash 持平**

---

### 2.4 MiniCPM-o 系列（端到端全模态模型，最强旗舰）

#### MiniCPM-o 2.6（GPT-4o 级多模态，2025.01）

首个真正意义上的端到端多模态手机模型，支持**图像、视频、文本、语音输入 + 文本、语音输出**。

- 2025 年 1 月登顶 GitHub Trending 和 Hugging Face Trending 第二名
- 采用 RLAIF-V 对齐技术（已获 CVPR 2025 Highlight）

#### MiniCPM-o 4.5（Gemini 2.5 Flash 级，2026.02 最新发布）

**参数总量：9B**（SigLip2 + Whisper-medium + CosyVoice2 + Qwen3-8B）

##### 三大核心能力

**🔥 视觉能力（Leading Visual）**
- OpenCompass 平均分 **77.6**，以 9B 参数**超越 GPT-4o、Gemini 2.0 Pro，逼近 Gemini 2.5 Flash**
- 支持 instruct 和 think 模式（单一模型内切换，灵活覆盖不同场景）

**🎙 语音能力（Strong Speech）**
- 支持**中英双语实时语音对话**，可配置音色
- 更自然、更有表现力、更稳定的语音对话
- 支持**声音克隆**（仅需一段参考音频，效果超越 CosyVoice2 等专业 TTS 工具）

**🎬 全双工多模态实时流（Full-Duplex Multimodal Live Streaming）**
- 业界首发：**输入流（视频+音频）与输出流（语音+文本）完全异步、不互相阻塞**
- 模型可以**同时看、听、说**，实现真正的实时全双工多模态对话
- **主动交互能力**：以 1Hz 频率主动决定是否说话，能主动发起提醒或评论
- 时间线毫秒级同步，通过**时分复用（TDM）机制**处理多模态流

##### 全双工架构要点

```
架构创新：
1. 离线模态编码/解码器 → 在线全双工流式处理
2. 语音 token 解码器交织处理文本/语音 token → 同步实时语音生成
3. 毫秒级时间线同步（输入视频/音频 + 输出文本/语音）
4. 系统提示分为：传统文本系统提示 + 音频系统提示（决定助理音色）
```

##### 部署需求

| 模式 | 设备要求 |
|------|---------|
| 全精度 PyTorch 推理（推荐） | NVIDIA GPU，**至少 28GB 显存** |
| llama.cpp-omni 半双工语音实时对话 | Apple M3/M4/M5（≥16GB RAM）或 NVIDIA GPU（≥12GB 显存） |
| llama.cpp-omni 全双工多模态流 | Apple M4 Max（≥24GB RAM）或 NVIDIA GPU（≥12GB 显存） |
| MiniCPM-V 4.0 GGUF CPU 推理 | 仅需 **4GB 内存** |

---

## 三、技术规格总览

### 3.1 模型参数对比

| 模型 | 参数量 | 上下文长度 | 多模态 | 许可 |
|------|--------|-----------|--------|------|
| MiniCPM-2B | 2B | 32K | ❌ | Apache-2.0 |
| MiniCPM3-4B | 4B | 32K | ❌ | Apache-2.0 |
| MiniCPM4-8B | 8B | 32K | ❌ | Apache-2.0 |
| MiniCPM4.1-8B | 8B | 64K（扩展至 128K） | ❌ | Apache-2.0 |
| MiniCPM-SALA | 9B | **1M token** | ❌ | Apache-2.0 |
| MiniCPM-V 4.0 | 4.1B | — | ✅ 图像/视频 | Apache-2.0 |
| MiniCPM-V 4.5 | ~8B | — | ✅ 图像/视频 | Apache-2.0 |
| MiniCPM-o 2.6 | ~9B | — | ✅ 图像/视频/语音/文本 | Apache-2.0 |
| MiniCPM-o 4.5 | 9B | — | ✅ 全模态输入+输出 | Apache-2.0 |

### 3.2 核心 benchmark 数据

#### 语言模型标准评测

## 四、推理框架与部署

### 4.1 支持的推理框架

| 框架 | 适用模型 | 特点 |
|------|---------|------|
| **HuggingFace Transformers** | 全部 | 最通用 |
| **vLLM** | MiniCPM4/4.1、SALA、MiniCPM-o/V | 高吞吐、内存高效 |
| **SGLang** | MiniCPM4/4.1、SALA、MiniCPM-o/V | 支持投机解码 |
| **CPM.cu** | MiniCPM4/4.1（推荐） | 稀疏注意力 + 量化 + 投机采样协同 |
| **llama.cpp** | MiniCPM4.1、MiniCPM-V 4.0/4.5 | CPU/本地推理，GGUF 格式 |
| **Ollama** | MiniCPM4.1、MiniCPM-V/MiniCPM-o | 一键运行 |
| **FlagOS** | MiniCPM-o | 统一多芯片后端 |

### 4.2 投机解码（Speculative Decoding）

MiniCPM4.1 支持 **EAGLE3 投机解码**：
- 草稿模型：MiniCPM4.1-8B-Eagle3
- 在 SGLang / vLLM 中均可配置
- **3 倍推理加速**（在深度推理模式下）

## 七、总结与评价

### 7.1 核心优势

1. **真正可商用的开源多模态模型**：Apache-2.0 无附加条件，手机端可跑
2. **稀疏注意力技术领先**：MiniCPM4.1 / SALA 系列在长上下文场景中同时实现了效果和效率的双重突破
3. **全模态端到端**：MiniCPM-o 4.5 的全双工多模态实时流是当前开源社区最前沿的探索
4. **完整工具链**：从训练（CPM.cu、SGLang、vLLM）到部署（llama.cpp、Ollama、FlagOS）全覆盖
5. **持续活跃**：2026 年 2 月仍有新模型发布，开发社区活跃（SOAR 大赛进行中）

### 7.2 适用场景

| 场景 | 推荐模型 |
|------|---------|
| 手机端离线图像理解 | MiniCPM-V 4.0（4.1B） |
| 高性能端侧图像/视频理解 | MiniCPM-V 4.5 |
| 端侧语音助手（实时对话） | MiniCPM-o 4.5 |
| 超长文档处理（100 万 token） | MiniCPM-SALA |
| 高效端侧推理（省资源） | MiniCPM4 + CPM.cu + BitCPM4 |
| 企业级多模态服务 | MiniCPM-o 4.5 + vLLM/SGLang |

### 7.3 与同类方案对比

| 维度 | MiniCPM 系列 | 典型竞品 |
|------|-------------|---------|
| 手机端运行 | ✅ 2B~9B 全系覆盖 | 大多数开源模型无法手机运行 |
| 100 万 token 上下文 | ✅ MiniCPM-SALA 唯一支持 | Qwen3-8B 等 1M 即 OOM |
| 语音对话 + 视觉 | ✅ MiniCPM-o 4.5 全双工 | 大多数 VLM 不支持语音 |
| 开源可商用 | ✅ Apache-2.0 | 部分模型有使用限制 |
| 中文优化 | ✅ 清华 & 人大联合研发 | 部分国外模型中文弱 |

arguments.content:
# MiniCPM 调研报告：端侧多模态大模型的演进与突破

**调研日期**：2026年3月29日
**数据来源**：GitHub / Hugging Face / ModelScope / 官方技术文档

---

## 一、项目概览

### 1.1 什么是 MiniCPM

MiniCPM 是由**清华大学 THUNLP 实验室**、**中国人民大学高灵学院（Gaoling School of AI）**与**模型最佳科技（ModelBest）**联合开发的**轻量级多模态大模型系列**。项目托管于 GitHub（OpenBMB/MiniCPM），采用 Apache-2.0 开源许可证，**免费可商用**。

MiniCPM 的核心理念是：**将 GPT-4V 级别的多模态能力压缩到能在手机端离线运行的模型体积**。从 2024 年 2 月发布首个 2B 参数版本至今，MiniCPM 已演化为一个涵盖纯语言模型（LLM）、视觉-语言模型（VLM）、端到端多模态模型（MiniCPM-o）的完整家族。

**里程碑时间线**

- **2024.02** — MiniCPM-2B 发布，性能对标 Mistral-7B
- **2024.04** — 发布 MiniCPM-2B-128K、MiniCPM-MoE-8x2B、MiniCPM-1B
- **2024.07** — 发布 MiniCPM-S-1B（87.89% FFN 稀疏度）
- **2024.09** — 发布 MiniCPM3-4B（超越 Phi-3.5-mini，逼近 Llama3.1-8B）
- **2025.06** — 发布 MiniCPM4（端侧 5 倍生成加速）
- **2025.09** — 发布 MiniCPM4.1 系列（混合推理 + 可训练稀疏注意力）
- **2026.02** — 发布 MiniCPM-SALA（100 万 token 上下文，稀疏+线性注意力混合架构）
- **2025.01/08** — 陆续发布 MiniCPM-o 2.6 / 4.5（端到端语音-视觉-文本多模态）

---

## 二、核心系列详解

### 2.1 MiniCPM 4 系列（语言模型）

#### MiniCPM4-8B / MiniCPM4-0.5B

MiniCPM4 是专为**端侧设备**优化的 LLM 系列，通过四个维度的系统创新实现效率突破：

| 维度 | 技术名称 | 说明 |
|------|---------|------|
| 模型架构 | **InfLLM-V2** 可训练稀疏注意力 | 在 128K 长文本中，每个 token 仅需计算与不到 5% token 的相关性 |
| 学习算法 | Model Wind Tunnel 2.0（高效可预测扩展）、BitCPM（三元量化，1.58 bit） | FP8 低精度计算 + Multi-token Prediction |
| 训练数据 | UltraClean（高质量预训练数据过滤）、UltraChat v2（高质量 SFT 数据） | 覆盖知识密集、推理、指令跟随、长文本等多维度 |
| 推理部署 | CPM.cu（轻量 CUDA 推理框架）、ArkInfer（跨平台部署） | 稀疏注意力 + 量化 + 投机采样协同 |

**性能亮点**：

- 在 Jetson AGX Orin 上，相比 Qwen3-8B 实现**约 7 倍解码速度提升**
- 在典型端侧芯片上，生成速度提升**超过 5 倍**
- 标准评测基准与同尺寸最优模型持平

#### MiniCPM4.1-8B（最新旗舰语言模型）

MiniCPM4.1 是**首个开源的可训练稀疏注意力推理 LLM**，支持**混合推理模式**（深度推理 / 非推理模式切换）。

**核心特性**：

- ✅ 稀疏注意力推理：每 token 只需计算少量相关性，大幅降低长文本计算开销
- ✅ 混合推理模式：通过 `enable_thinking=True/False` 或在对话末尾加 `/think` / `/no_think` 动态切换
- ✅ 深度推理模式：**3 倍解码加速**（相比标准推理）
- ✅ 支持上下文最长 64K，原生支持 LongRoPE 扩展至 128K

**推理框架**：HuggingFace Transformers / vLLM / SGLang / **CPM.cu**（官方推荐，用于稀疏推理）

#### BitCPM4 系列（三元量化）

BitCPM4 通过**量化感知训练（QAT）**将模型参数压缩至仅 **1.58 bit**（3 值），性能与全精度同尺寸模型相当，同时训练效率大幅提升。

---

### 2.2 MiniCPM-SALA（前沿稀疏架构，2026.02 最新发布）

**MiniCPM-SALA** 是 2026 年 2 月 11 日发布的重磅新模型，全称 **Sparse Attention and Linear Attention**，是**首个有效融合稀疏注意力和线性注意力**的大规模混合模型，专为百万 token 上下文场景设计。

#### 架构创新

- **25% 稀疏注意力层**（InfLLM-V2）：保留高精度局部细节建模
- **75% 线性注意力层**（Lightning Attention）：全局高效计算
- **HyPE（Hybrid Positional Embedding）**：协调短上下文和长上下文性能
- **HALO Adaptation**：通过层优化将密集注意力能力蒸馏到混合架构

#### 性能数据

**效率评测（A6000D / RTX 5090）**：

| 指标 | MiniCPM-SALA (9B) | Qwen3-8B（对比） |
|------|------------------|-----------------|
| 首 token 时间（TTFT） | 最高 2.5 倍加速 | baseline |
| 128K 上下文推理速度 | 3.5 倍加速 | OOM 失败 |
| 1M token 上下文 | ✅ RTX 5090 单卡运行 | ❌ OOM |
| RULER / NoLiMa（128K） | 最高分 | 低于 SALA |
| 2048K 超长上下文得分 | **81.6**（训练仅用 520K token） | 不支持 |

**标准评测基准（平均分）**：MiniCPM-SALA 平均分 **76.53**，超越 Qwen3-8B 和 Falcon-H1R-7B。

> 🔥 **SOAR 2026 大赛**（稀疏算子加速挑战赛）正在进行，NVIDIA 6000D 环境，奖金池超 10 万美元，目标是优化 9B 规模、100 万 token 推理性能。

---

### 2.3 MiniCPM-V 系列（视觉-语言模型）

#### MiniCPM-V 4.0（2025 年最新图像理解模型）

基于 **SigLIP2-400M 视觉编码器 + MiniCPM4-3B 语言基座**，总参数仅 **4.1B**。

**核心指标**：

- OpenCompass 8 大基准平均得分：**69.0**（超越 GPT-4.1-mini-20250414、MiniCPM-V 2.6、Qwen2.5-VL-3B）
- **iPhone 16 Pro Max** 实测：首 token 延迟 < 2秒，解码速度 > 17 token/s，无发热问题
- 支持 llama.cpp / Ollama / vLLM / SGLang / LLaMA-Factory / 本地 Web Demo
- 已开源 iOS App，支持 iPhone / iPad 离线运行

#### MiniCPM-V 4.5（超越 GPT-4o 的视觉能力，2025.08）

**核心指标**：

- OpenCompass 平均分：**77.6**
- 在多项基准上**超越 GPT-4o-latest、Gemini-2.0 Pro、Qwen2.5-VL 72B**
- 支持高分辨率图像（最高 180 万像素）、高帧率视频（最高 10fps，任意宽高比）
- OmniDocBench 端到端英文文档解析：**超越 Gemini-3 Flash、GPT-5、DeepSeek-OCR 2**
- 支持 30+ 语言多语言能力
- 可信行为（MMHal-Bench）：**与 Gemini 2.5 Flash 持平**

---

### 2.4 MiniCPM-o 系列（端到端全模态模型，最强旗舰）

#### MiniCPM-o 2.6（GPT-4o 级多模态，2025.01）

首个真正意义上的端到端多模态手机模型，支持**图像、视频、文本、语音输入 + 文本、语音输出**。

- 2025 年 1 月登顶 GitHub Trending 和 Hugging Face Trending 第二名
- 采用 RLAIF-V 对齐技术（已获 CVPR 2025 Highlight）

#### MiniCPM-o 4.5（Gemini 2.5 Flash 级，2026.02 最新发布）

**参数总量：9B**（SigLip2 + Whisper-medium + CosyVoice2 + Qwen3-8B）

##### 三大核心能力

**🔥 视觉能力（Leading Visual）**

- OpenCompass 平均分 **77.6**，以 9B 参数**超越 GPT-4o、Gemini 2.0 Pro，逼近 Gemini 2.5 Flash**
- 支持 instruct 和 think 模式（单一模型内切换，灵活覆盖不同场景）

**🎙 语音能力（Strong Speech）**

- 支持**中英双语实时语音对话**，可配置音色
- 更自然、更有表现力、更稳定的语音对话
- 支持**声音克隆**（仅需一段参考音频，效果超越 CosyVoice2 等专业 TTS 工具）

**🎬 全双工多模态实时流（Full-Duplex Multimodal Live Streaming）**

- 业界首发：**输入流（视频+音频）与输出流（语音+文本）完全异步、不互相阻塞**
- 模型可以**同时看、听、说**，实现真正的实时全双工多模态对话
- **主动交互能力**：以 1Hz 频率主动决定是否说话，能主动发起提醒或评论
- 时间线毫秒级同步，通过**时分复用（TDM）机制**处理多模态流

##### 部署需求

| 模式 | 设备要求 |
|------|---------|
| 全精度 PyTorch 推理（推荐） | NVIDIA GPU，**至少 28GB 显存** |
| llama.cpp-omni 半双工语音实时对话 | Apple M3/M4/M5（≥16GB RAM）或 NVIDIA GPU（≥12GB 显存） |
| llama.cpp-omni 全双工多模态流 | Apple M4 Max（≥24GB RAM）或 NVIDIA GPU（≥12GB 显存） |
| MiniCPM-V 4.0 GGUF CPU 推理 | 仅需 **4GB 内存** |

---

## 三、技术规格总览

### 3.1 模型参数对比

| 模型 | 参数量 | 上下文长度 | 多模态 | 许可 |
|------|--------|-----------|--------|------|
| MiniCPM-2B | 2B | 32K | ❌ | Apache-2.0 |

# Feishu Chat File Delivery

This skill is for direct file delivery into a Feishu chat message.

## Routing rule

If the user asks to send a file to the current conversation, current chat, chat box, or another Feishu chat, this skill takes priority over any doc-publishing workflow.

Only switch to `feishu_doc upload_file` when one of these is true:

1. The user explicitly asks for a Feishu document or wiki
2. Direct chat delivery has already been attempted and failed
3. The user explicitly accepts a document-link fallback

## Critical constraints

1. Do not use `feishu_chat` for file sending. That tool only supports chat metadata actions such as `info` and `members`.
2. Direct file delivery must go through outbound media delivery, which requires the assistant reply to include a `MEDIA:` line.
3. Local file paths are only readable by OpenClaw when they are inside approved local roots.

## Approved local roots on this machine

Use one of these locations for direct file delivery:

- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace`
- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\media`
- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\agents`

Do not output `MEDIA:` with files that stay only under repo paths such as `E:\projects\clawbot\...`, because outbound media loading may reject them as unsafe local paths.

## Preferred workflow

When the user asks to send a local PDF/Word file directly to the current Feishu conversation:

1. Confirm the source file exists.
2. If the source file is outside approved local roots, copy it into `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox\` first.
3. Reply with short confirmation text plus a dedicated `MEDIA:` line using the copied absolute path.

## Required reply format

Use plain text like this:

```text
已将调研报告发送到当前飞书会话。
MEDIA: "C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox\research-report.pdf"
```

Notes:

- Put the file path on its own `MEDIA:` line.
- Quote the path when it contains spaces.
- For multiple files, use multiple `MEDIA:` lines or one line with multiple valid media paths.
- Keep any explanation outside code fences.

## Fallback behavior

If direct media delivery is unsafe, unsupported, or fails:

1. Create a Feishu doc or wiki page.
2. Upload the file with `feishu_doc` `action: "upload_file"`.
3. Reply to the chat with the doc link and clearly say it was attached to a document, not directly sent as a chat file.

## Good response pattern

- If direct send succeeds: short confirmation + `MEDIA:` line.
- If fallback is needed: explain that direct chat file delivery was not completed and that the file was attached to a Feishu Doc/Wiki instead.
